senses.definition: the structure of a building, piece of furniture, vehicle, etc. that supports and shapes it
the main exterior structure of a vehicle that includes the doors, roof, and panels

examples.x: The car's body was made of lightweight aluminum.
She chose a car with a sleek and aerodynamic body.
The body was painted a glossy red.
They inspected the body for rust and damage.
He noticed a dent in the body after the minor collision.

senses.definition: the outer body structure of a vehicle, encompassing components like panels, doors, hood, trunk, and roof

examples.x: The sports car's bodywork boasts sleek, aerodynamic lines.
Classic cars often feature iconic bodywork designs.
They repainted the vehicle due to scratched bodywork.
Custom truck bodywork can include lifted suspensions and unique paint.
They specialize in restoring vintage car bodywork to its original glory.

senses.definition: the frame of a vehicle that supports the body and other components

examples.x: The chassis of the truck was built to handle heavy loads.
She examined the chassis for signs of rust.
He upgraded the chassis with reinforced steel.
They lifted the car to inspect the chassis.
The chassis provided a sturdy foundation for the vehicle.

senses.definition: the structure that creates the outer top part of a vehicle, building, etc.

examples.x: He placed solar panels on the roof to generate electricity.
She climbed onto the roof to enjoy the view from above.
She noticed a small bird nesting on the roof of her garage.
She sat on the roof of her car to enjoy a picnic under the stars.
The snow on the roof started to melt in the warmth of the sun.

senses.definition: the structural and cosmetic components located at the forward-facing exterior of a vehicle
the back portion or tail section of a vehicle
a central shaft for a rotating wheel or gear, crucial for vehicle movement

examples.x: The front axle needed replacement due to wear.
She heard a noise coming from the rear axle.
He greased the axle to ensure smooth rotation.
The axle was bent after hitting the pothole.
They balanced the wheels on the axle for better performance.

senses.definition: a metal bar or structure installed in a vehicle to protect passengers in case of a rollover

examples.x: The rally car had a sturdy roll bar installed to enhance driver safety during high-speed races.
It's important for off-road vehicles to have a roll bar to minimize injuries in case of accidents.
She felt secure driving the convertible knowing that it had a roll bar to protect her in emergencies.
Mechanics often recommend reinforcing the roll bar after significant modifications to ensure it meets safety standards.
The classic car collector insisted on restoring the vintage vehicle with an authentic roll bar for historical accuracy.

senses.definition: a suspension component that connects the left and right sides of a vehicle's suspension to reduce body roll and enhance stability when cornering

examples.x: When a car turns sharply, the anti-roll bar limits the amount the vehicle leans, keeping it more level and stable.
Anti-roll bars are common in both performance vehicles and everyday cars for their ability to enhance driving dynamics.
Adjusting the stiffness of an anti-roll bar can fine-tune a vehicle's balance between comfort and responsiveness on different road surfaces.
In racing, anti-roll bars are crucial for maintaining optimal grip and stability through high-speed corners.
Off-road vehicles use disconnectable anti-roll bars to improve wheel articulation over challenging terrain.

senses.definition: a strong metal bar added to vehicles to protect passengers by stopping things from breaking in during an accident
a device in a vehicle's exhaust system that reduces the emission of harmful pollutants by promoting chemical reactions that convert them into less harmful substances

examples.x: The catalytic converter plays a crucial role in reducing harmful emissions from automobiles by converting pollutants into less harmful compounds.
Legislation mandates the use of catalytic converters in vehicles to meet environmental standards for air quality.
Mechanics can diagnose catalytic converter issues through specialized tests to ensure proper functioning.
The chemical reactions within the catalytic converter help transform nitrogen oxides into nitrogen and oxygen.
Hybrid vehicles often use advanced catalytic converters to further minimize their environmental impact.

senses.definition: the container that holds the fuel for a vehicle

examples.x: He filled the gas tank before the long trip.
They replaced the old gas tank with a new one.
She smelled gasoline and suspected a leak in the gas tank.
The gas tank had a capacity of 15 gallons.
The gas tank was located under the rear seat.

senses.definition: a storage unit in a vehicle for fluids like coolant, brake fluid, or windshield washer fluid

examples.x: The coolant reservoir tank helps regulate engine temperature by expanding and contracting with heat cycles.
The brake fluid reservoir tank ensures proper braking function by storing hydraulic fluid for the braking system.
The windshield washer fluid reservoir tank holds cleaning solution to keep the windshield clear of debris and dirt.
Each reservoir tank serves a critical role in maintaining vehicle performance and safety by ensuring essential fluids are readily available.
The oil reservoir tank in the vehicle stores and distributes lubricating oil to ensure smooth operation of the engine components.

senses.definition: the system in a vehicle that delivers power from the engine to the wheels

examples.x: He upgraded the power train for better performance.
They serviced the power train to fix the issue.
The power train warranty covered major repairs.
She noticed a vibration in the power train during acceleration.
The power train includes the engine, transmission, and drivetrain.

senses.definition: the part of a vehicle that uses a particular fuel to make the vehicle move

examples.x: The mechanic repaired the engine of the car, which had been making strange noises.
The airplane's engine roared to life as it prepared for takeoff.
She studied mechanical engineering to learn how to design and build engines.
The boat's engine failed, leaving them stranded in the middle of the lake.
The new electric car features a powerful engine that provides fast acceleration.

senses.definition: a machine that converts any form of energy into mechanical energy

examples.x: He repaired the motor in his car to improve its performance.
The windmill uses a motor to generate electricity from wind energy.
The blender's motor stopped working after years of heavy use.

senses.definition: the system that transmits power from the engine to the wheels, allowing the vehicle to change speeds

examples.x: The transmission needed a fluid change.
She shifted the transmission into gear.
He preferred a manual transmission for more control.
They repaired the transmission to fix the slipping gears.
The transmission was smooth and responsive.

senses.definition: a type of brake that uses calipers to squeeze pairs of pads against a disc to create friction

examples.x: The car had disc brakes on all four wheels.
She felt the stopping power of the disc brakes.
They inspected the disc brakes for wear and tear.
The disc brakes provided reliable performance in all conditions.
He replaced the worn-out disc brakes with new ones.

senses.definition: a component of disc brakes that presses against the rotor to create friction and stop the vehicle

examples.x: The brake pads needed replacement due to wear.
She heard a squealing noise from the brake pads.
He checked the thickness of the brake pads.
The brake pads were designed to minimize noise and dust.
They installed high-performance brake pads for better stopping power.

senses.definition: any of the circular objects typically found under vehicles like cars, bicycles, buses, etc., used to make movement possible by turning

examples.x: The car’s wheels were spinning fast as it accelerated.
She changed the wheel on her bicycle after it got flat.
They replaced the old wheels with new ones for better performance.
The wheels on the toy truck were small but functional.
The wheels of the truck made a screeching sound as it stopped.
The mechanic inspected the wheels to ensure they were aligned.

senses.definition: a circular rubber object that covers the wheel of a vehicle

examples.x: She noticed the tread on her tires was worn down and decided it was time for a replacement.
The car's tires were designed to provide good traction in both wet and dry conditions.
He rotated the tires regularly to ensure even wear and extend their lifespan.
She bought new all-season tires to improve handling and safety in various weather conditions.
He changed the tire on his bike before the race.

senses.definition: the outer edge of a wheel that holds the tire

examples.x: She cleaned the rims to remove dirt and brake dust.
The rim was bent after hitting the curb.
He upgraded to alloy rims for a sportier look.
They balanced the tires on the rims for smooth driving.
The rims were designed to be both strong and lightweight.

senses.definition: the part of a tire that comes into contact with the road, featuring patterns for grip

examples.x: They rotated the tires to ensure even tread wear.
The tread provided excellent traction on wet roads.
He chose tires with an aggressive tread pattern for off-roading.
The tread on the tires was worn down and needed replacement.

senses.definition: a type of tire construction where the cords run at right angles to the direction of travel

examples.x: She preferred radial tires for their durability.
He noticed a smoother ride with the new radial tires.
They recommended radial tires for all-season use.
The car was equipped with radial tires for better performance.
The radial tire construction provided better traction and handling.
